node_impl.hpp

Go to the documentation of this file.
00001 
00005 #include "sprite/visit.hpp"
00006 #include <boost/pool/object_pool.hpp>
00007 
00008 //==============================================
00009 // Implementation of Node::new and Node::delete.
00010 //==============================================
00011 namespace sprite
00012 {
00013   // This memory pool calls the node object destructors upon destruction.
00014   extern boost::object_pool<AbstractNode> node_allocator;
00015 
00016   inline void * Node::operator new(size_t bytes)
00017   {
00018     // Bare Node objects should not be allocated directly.  Instead, a concrete
00019     // instance of Node_<Payload> should be allocated.
00020     assert(sizeof(AbstractNode) == bytes);
00021     void * px = reinterpret_cast<void *>(node_allocator.malloc());
00022 
00023     // Note: a custom raw memory allocator would need to be specified to
00024     // object_pool to meet this requirement.
00025 
00026     #if 0
00027     // We're aiming for exactly 2 nodes per cache line (on modern x86_64
00028     // hardware).  Verify the alignment to ensure that nodes don't span cache
00029     // line boundaries.
00030     assert(reinterpret_cast<size_t>(px) % NODE_BYTES == 0);
00031     #endif
00032     return px;
00033 
00034   }
00035   inline void Node::operator delete(void * p)
00036     { node_allocator.free(reinterpret_cast<AbstractNode*>(p)); }
00037 }
00038 
00039 //==============================
00040 // Implementation of Node::iter.
00041 //==============================
00042 namespace sprite
00043 {
00044   namespace visitors
00045   {
00047     struct Iter : static_visitor<Node::range_type>
00048     {
00049       // Handle node types with children.
00050       result_type operator()(InPlaceNode1 & node) const
00051       {
00052         return result_type(
00053             &node.payload.arg, &node.payload.arg + 1
00054           );
00055       }
00056       result_type operator()(InPlaceNode2 & node) const
00057       {
00058         return result_type(
00059             &node.payload.arg0, &node.payload.arg1 + 1
00060           );
00061       }
00062       result_type operator()(ChildListNode & node) const
00063       { 
00064         payloads::ChildList const & list = node.payload;
00065         return result_type(&list.args()[0], &list.args()[node.arity()]);
00066       }
00067 
00068       // Handle node types without children.
00069       template<typename NodeType>
00070       result_type operator()(NodeType &) const
00071       {
00072         // Build an empty range without using singular pointers.
00073         static NodePtr tmp;
00074         return result_type(&tmp,&tmp);
00075       }
00076     };
00077   }
00078 
00079   // Normally, the const implementation would be shared by using const_cast to
00080   // cast the result back, but in this case there's no suitable const_cast for
00081   // const_range_type to range_type.  Instead, the non-const implementation is
00082   // shared, so the above visitor really shouldn't modify anything.
00083 
00084   inline Node::range_type Node::iter()
00085   {
00086     static visitors::Iter const visitor;
00087     return visit(visitor, *this);
00088   }
00089   inline Node::const_range_type Node::iter() const
00090   {
00091     static visitors::Iter const visitor;
00092     return visit(visitor, const_cast<Node &>(*this));
00093   }
00094 }
00095 
00096 //====================================
00097 // Implementation of Node::operator[].
00098 //====================================
00099 namespace sprite
00100 {
00101   namespace visitors
00102   {
00104     struct At : static_visitor<NodePtr const &>
00105     {
00106       // A forwarding node should never be obtained for use here.
00107       result_type operator()(FwdNode const &, size_t) const
00108         { throw RuntimeError("Unexpected forward node."); }
00109 
00110       // Handle node types with children.
00111       result_type operator()(InPlaceNode1 const & node, size_t i) const
00112       {
00113         assert(i<1);
00114         return node.payload.arg;
00115       }
00116       result_type operator()(InPlaceNode2 const & node, size_t i) const
00117       {
00118         switch(i)
00119         {
00120           case 0: return node.payload.arg0;
00121           case 1: return node.payload.arg1;
00122           default: throw RuntimeError("Child index is out of range.");
00123         }
00124       }
00125       result_type operator()(ChildListNode const & node, size_t i) const
00126       { 
00127         assert(i<node.arity());
00128         return node.payload.args()[i];
00129       }
00130 
00131       // Handle node types without children.
00132       template<typename NodeType>
00133       result_type operator()(NodeType const &, size_t) const
00134         { throw RuntimeError("Child index is out of range."); }
00135     };
00136   }
00137 
00138   inline NodePtr & Node::operator[](size_t i)
00139   {
00140     return const_cast<NodePtr &>(
00141         static_cast<Node const *>(this)->operator[](i)
00142       );
00143   }
00144   inline NodePtr const & Node::operator[](size_t i) const
00145   {
00146     static visitors::At const visitor;
00147     return visit(tr1::bind<NodePtr const &>(visitor, _1, i), *this);
00148   }
00149 }
00150 
00151 //==================================
00152 // Implementation of Node::position.
00153 //==================================
00154 namespace sprite
00155 {
00156   namespace visitors
00157   {
00159     struct Position : static_visitor<size_t>
00160     {
00161       // Handle node types with children.
00162       result_type operator()(
00163           InPlaceNode1 const & node, NodePtr const & child
00164         ) const
00165       {
00166         assert(&node.payload.arg == &child);
00167         return 0;
00168       }
00169       result_type operator()(
00170           InPlaceNode2 const & node, NodePtr const & child
00171         ) const
00172       {
00173         size_t const i = &child - &node.payload.arg0;
00174         assert(i<2);
00175         return i;
00176       }
00177       result_type operator()(
00178           ChildListNode const & node, NodePtr const & child
00179         ) const
00180       { 
00181         size_t const i = &child - &node.payload.args()[0];
00182         assert(i<node.arity());
00183         return i;
00184       }
00185 
00186       // Handle node types without children.
00187       template<typename NodeType>
00188       result_type operator()(NodeType const &, NodePtr const &) const
00189         { throw RuntimeError("Invalid parent node."); }
00190     };
00191   }
00192 
00193   inline size_t Node::position(NodePtr const & child) const
00194   {
00195     static visitors::Position const visitor;
00196     return visit(tr1::bind<size_t>(visitor, _1, tr1::cref(child)), *this);
00197   }
00198 }
00199 
00200 //===============================
00201 // Implementation of Node::clone.
00202 //===============================
00203 namespace sprite
00204 {
00205   namespace visitors
00206   {
00208     struct Clone : static_visitor<NodePtr>
00209     {
00210       template<typename NodeType>
00211       result_type operator()(NodeType const & node) const
00212       {
00213         NodePtr p(new NodeType(node));
00214         return p;
00215       }
00216     };
00217   }
00218 
00219   inline NodePtr Node::clone()
00220   {
00221     static visitors::Clone const visitor;
00222     return visit(visitor, *this);
00223   }
00224 }
00225 
00226 //=========================================
00227 // Implementation of Node::destroy_payload.
00228 //=========================================
00229 namespace sprite
00230 {
00231   namespace visitors
00232   {
00234     struct DestroyPayload : static_visitor<void>
00235     {
00236       template<typename Payload>
00237       result_type operator()(Node_<Payload> & node) const
00238         { node.payload.~Payload(); }
00239     };
00240   }
00241 
00242   inline void Node::destroy_payload()
00243   {
00244     static visitors::DestroyPayload const visitor;
00245     return visit(visitor, *this);
00246   }
00247 }
